statement.markdown: Given an array of integers and a target subarray, determine whether the target subarray appears contiguously within the original array. If it does, return the index of the start of the first occurrence of the target subarray. If it does not appear, return -1. Note that the target subarray may be in reverse order.
statement.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains an integer n, the size of the input array. The second line contains n space-separated integers representing the input array. The third line contains the target subarray.
statement.outputFormat: Return the index of the start of the first occurrence of the target subarray, or -1 if it does not appear.
statement.notes: The input array may contain duplicates, and the target subarray may not appear at all.

constraints.description: Target subarray, possibly in reverse order

examples.explanation: The target subarray 2 1 appears at the start of the input array.
The target subarray 3 2 1 appears at the start of the input array, possibly in reverse order.
The target subarray 1 2 does not appear at all in the input array.

functionSignature.python: def solve(self, nums: list[int], target: str) -> int:
